source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/05_해외 등록 서류 (영업팀 F-up)/260213 태국 MD Healthcard Co., Ltd/260327 추가보완서류/1.2 [Portable X-ray system] Technical Document/[Attachment] 4. Summary of Design Verification and Validation/4.1 Laboratory ISO17025 Cetificate/2. [KES_Co.,_Ltd] ISOIEC 17025 Certificate.pdf

sourceSummary.oneLine: Accreditation scope text for certificate No. KT489 listing electrical, medical device, EMC, energy efficiency, environmental and reliability test methods with test ranges and site/field testing status.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Use to identify whether the laboratory scope includes specific standards and test ranges relevant to medical electrical equipment, X-ray equipment, EMC, IP testing, and related electrical safety testing.
keyPoints: The document repeatedly identifies certificate or scope number No. KT489.
The scope includes 03. Electrical Testing categories such as Medical devices and EMC (Electromagnetic Compatibility).
Medical device scope includes IEC/EN 60601 and IEC/ISO 80601 series standards.
X-ray related medical electrical equipment standards list X-ray test ranges including 35 to 160 kVp, 0.2 mA to 2 A, 0.001 s to 999 s, and measuring distance 5.5 m.

Most medical electrical safety entries list test voltage Max. 600 V, test power Max. 5 kVA, dielectric strength Max. AC 5 kV or DC 6 kV, insulation resistance Max. 5 GΩ, and ground continuity Max. 60 A.

evidence.claim: The source is a certificate or scope identified by No. KT489.
The document covers electrical testing scope.
The scope includes medical devices as a materials/products category.
IEC 60601-1 is included for medical electrical equipment basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1:2005 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment – Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

evidence.claim: EN 60601-1 is included for medical electrical equipment basic safety and essential performance.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 60601-1:2006 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment – Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance

evidence.claim: The IEC 60601-1 medical device electrical safety scope has exceptions including unwanted radiation hazards, fire prevention, sterilization, radiotherapy equipment, diagnostic or therapeutic radiation, and lithium batteries.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1:2005 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment – Part 1: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance (Exception) Cl. 10 Protection against unwanted and excessive radiation HAZARDS Cl. 11.2 Fire prevention Cl. 11.6.7 Sterilization Cl. 12.4.5.3 Radiotherapy equipment Cl. 12.4.5.4 Other ME EQUIPMENT producing diagnostic or therapeutic radiation Cl. 15.4.3.4 Primary Lithium batteries

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-1-3 is included for radiation protection in diagnostic X-ray equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-3:2008/AMD1:2013 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-3: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral Standard: Radiation protection in diagnostic X-ray equipment
evidence.pageTarget: IEC 60601-1-3 Diagnostic X Ray Radiation Protection

evidence.claim: The X-ray test range for IEC 60601-1-3 includes 35 to 160 kVp, 0.2 mA to 2 A, 0.001 s to 999 s, 10 nGy to 9,999 Gy, and measuring distance 5.5 m.
evidence.evidenceText: X-ray: (35 to 160) kVp, 0.2 mA to 2 A, 0.001s to 999 s, 10 nGy to 9,999 Gy, (1.0 to 14.0) mmAl, 0.00 μGy/h to 0.1 Gy/h Measuring distance: 5.5 m

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-2-54 is included for X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-2-54:2022 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment - Part 2-54: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of X-ray equipment for radiography and radioscopy

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-2-28 is included for X-ray tube assemblies for medical diagnosis.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-2-28:2017 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment - Part 2-28: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of X-ray tube assemblies for medical diagnosis

evidence.claim: IEC 60601-2-63 and IEC 60601-2-65 are included for dental extra-oral and intra-oral X-ray equipment.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-2-63:2012/AMD2:2021 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment - Part 2-63: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of dental extra-oral X-ray equipment ... IEC 60601-2-65:2012/AMD2:2021 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment - Part 2-65: Particular requirements for the basic safety and essential performance of dental intra-oral X-ray equipment

evidence.claim: The medical device scope includes IEC 60601-1-2 and EN 60601-1-2 for electromagnetic compatibility or electromagnetic disturbances requirements and tests.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60601-1-2:2014+A1:2020 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment – Part 1-2: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance – Collateral Standard: Electromagnetic disturbances – Requirements and tests

evidence.evidenceText: KS C IEC 60601-1-2:2020 Medical devices Medical electrical equipment - Part 1-2: General requirements for basic safety and essential performance - Collateral standard: Electromagnetic disturbances - Requirements and tests

evidence.claim: The EMC section includes conducted emissions, radiated emissions, ESD, radiated susceptibility, EFT/burst, surge, conducted susceptibility, magnetic field, and voltage dip ranges for medical device EMC entries.
evidence.evidenceText: CE: 0.15 ㎒ ~ 30 ㎒ RE: 9 ㎑ ~ 18 ㎓ ESD: ± 15 ㎸ RS: 80 ㎒ ~ 6 ㎓ EFT/Burst: ± 2 ㎸ Surge: ± 2 ㎸ CS: 0.15 ㎒ ~ 80 ㎒, 10 V Magnetic field: 30 A/m V-dip: (0 ~ 100) %

evidence.claim: EN 61000-4-39 and IEC 61000-4-39 are included for radiated fields in close proximity immunity testing.
evidence.evidenceText: EN 61000-4-39:2017 Medical devices Electromagnetic compatibility (EMC) - Part 4-39: Testing and measurement techniques - Radiated fields in close proximity - Immunity test

evidence.claim: IP Code enclosure protection testing is included, with IPX9 excepted and IP1X through IP6X and IPX3 through IPX8 listed.
evidence.evidenceText: IEC 60529:1989+A1:1999+A2:2013 Electronic equipment, components or articles Degrees of protection provided by enclosures (IP Code) (Exception) IPX9 IP1X, IP2X, IP3X, IP4X, IP5X, IP6X IPX3, IPX4, IPX5, IPX6, IPX7, IPX8

evidence.claim: The scope includes wired/wireless communication device testing for human exposure to electromagnetic fields and SAR-related standards.
evidence.evidenceText: 03. Electrical Testing 03.008 Wired/wireless communication devices ... Human exposure to radio frequency fields from hand-held and body-mounted wireless communication devices ... specific absorption rate (SAR)

evidence.claim: Energy efficiency testing scope includes US 10 CFR Part 430 appendices for televisions, battery chargers, and external power supplies.
evidence.evidenceText: 10 CFR Part 430 Subpart B, Appendix H Television Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (Uniform Test Method for Measuring the Power Consumption of Television Sets) ... Appendix Y ... Battery Chargers ... Appendix Z ... External Power Supplies

evidence.claim: Environmental and reliability testing scope includes IEC 60068 series environmental tests and IEC 60529 IP Code testing.
evidence.evidenceText: 03. Electrical Testing 03.014 Environmental and Reliability ... IEC 60068-2-14:2023 ... IEC 60068-2-1:2025 ... IEC 60529:1989+A1:1999+A2:2013

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the provided source text.
Revision or version of the certificate itself is not present; only standards revisions and amendments are listed.
Issuing accreditation authority or jurisdiction is not explicitly shown in the provided source text.
Product model is not identified in the provided source text; source path mentions Portable X-ray system but no model is shown.
OCR/text quality issues are present, including misspellings such as Measruing, Floatiing, prited, equipmet, and replacement characters in temperature ranges.
The source text begins at page 2/174 and appears to be an excerpt of a longer certificate or accreditation scope rather than the full cover page.
